texts.full_text: of myocardial energy metabolism. The effects of equipotent doses in negative inotropic and chronotropic properties of nipradilol [10 micrograms/kg/min intravenously (i.v.)] and propranolol (20 micrograms/kg/min i.v.) on hemodynamics and energy metabolites in ischemic hearts were examined in anesthetized dogs. After 5-min infusion of these agent, the heart was rapidly excised, and transmural biopsies were obtained from the subendocardial, midmyocardial, and subepicardial layers of the left ventricular wall. The levels of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), adenosine diphosphate (ADP), adenosine monophosphate (AMP), creatine phosphate (CP), and lactate were measured using high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC). The results showed that nipradilol significantly improved the ischemic derangement of myocardial energy metabolism compared to propranolol. Specifically, nipradilol maintained higher levels of ATP and CP, and lower levels of ADP, AMP, and lactate in the ischemic myocardium. These findings suggest that the vasodilating property of nipradilol plays a significant role in preserving myocar

texts.full_text: Additionally, parole agents failed to act when GPS showed Garrido made regular trips that took him more than 25 miles from his home in violation of his parole. In Garrido's case, GPS monitoring appeared to be largely a formality – a finding that led the inspector general to conclude that "the current passive GPS monitoring program appears to provide little, if any, value to proactive parole supervision." The use of GPS to track sex offenders – theoretically to prevent them from committing other crimes – has been questioned before. "GPS monitoring – embraced as a simple technological solution for tracking the whereabouts of convicted sex offenders – is proving to be something less than a silver bullet for state and local public safety agencies," wrote Jim McKay in a February article in Government Technology . id: 5900f38c1000cf542c50fe9f challengeType: 5 title: 'Problem 32: Pandigital products' forumTopicId: 301976 localeTitle: 'Проблема 32: Продукты Pandigital' --- ## Description
Мы будем говорить, что n-значное число является pandigital, если оно использует все цифры от 1 до n ровно один раз; например, 5-значное число, 15234, составляет от 1 до 5 pandigital.

Продукт 7254 необычен, так как идентичность 39 × 186 = 7254, содержащая мультипликацию, множитель и продукт от 1 до 9 pandigital.

Найдите сумму всех продуктов, чей идентификатор multipicand / multiplier / product может быть записан как от 1 до 9 pandigital.

СОВЕТ. Некоторые продукты могут быть получены более чем одним способом, поэтому обязательно включайте их только один раз в свою сумму.

texts.full_text: family Orchidae. The plant hails from Thailand, where it was first discovered and described by botanical explorers due to its unique features that distinguish this specific variety within Bulbophyllum genus. Bulbs of 'romburghii' are relatively small compared with other species found across different geographical regions in Southeast Asia but what they lack for stature is more than made up through their vibrant and colorful orchid flowers. These striking blooms come primarily as a resultant from the union between two or sometimes three bracts, which then transform into petals. One of its most notable characteristics are those slender flower spikes that carry each successive bloom in succession towards either end (the distal tip) with their coloration often transitiOning along this axis. These flowers' fragrant aroma has also earned them a special recognition among orchid enthusiasts, contributing to the global interest and popularity for Bulbophyllum species as ornamental plants. Blade currently plays for Irish provincial side Connacht in the Pro14, having signed a senior contract ahead of the 2015–16 season. Early life Blade grew up in Monivea, a village in County Galway. He attended Athenry Vocational School in nearby Athenry. In addition to rugby, Blade also played Gaelic football and hurling at under-age level. Blade started out in rugby with local junior club Monivea, where his father and brother have also played at scrum-half. He played with them until 2013, moving to All-Ireland League club Galwegians after entering Connacht's academy. Career Connacht Having already played for the province's second tier side, the Connacht Eagles, Blade joined Connacht's academy at the start of the 22014–15 season. He was elevated to senior squad status during this time and made his professional debut in a Guinness Pro12 match against Munster on November 30, scoring twice. Blade has been an integral part of Connacht's team, contributing heavily both at the scrum-half position as well through kicks from goal. His strong work ethic and determination were critical factors in his development into a dependable player forConnhte province. He played prominently during their 2017–18 Pro14 season, finishing second to Leinster at the end of that campaign with Connacht. Blade was part of several key moments throughout this term which saw him earn a reputation as one ofConnht's top players. In 2018, he received recognition for his hard work and dedication by being named Connacht Rugby Player Of The Year. texts.full_text: the Catskills and the Shawangunk Ridge, where it goes over High Falls and finally out to the Hudson at Kingston, receiving along the way the Wallkill River. The name of Rondout Creek comes from the fort, or redoubt, that was erected near its mouth. The Dutch equivalent of the English word redoubt (meaning a fort or stronghold) is reduyt. In the Dutch records of Wildwyck, however, the spelling used to designate this same fort is invariably Ronduyt during the earliest period, with the present form rondout (often capitalized) appearing as early as November 22, 1666. The Roundout Creek became economically important in the 19th century when the Delaware and Hudson Canal followed closely alongside it from its source to Kingston. This canal was constructed between 1828 and 1831 primarily for transporting anthracite coal from mines near Carbondale, Pennsylvania into New York City via barge traffic through North River (now known as the Harlem River) canals. The Delaware and Hudson Canal boosted economic activity along Rondout Creek by enabling efficient trade routes. The construction of railroads further enhanced transportation opportunities in this region during later years when trains began replacing canal barges after 1878, though remnants like High Falls Park now preserve some history through scenic views over the waterfalls where once goods were loaded and unloaded. With time industrial operations waned along Rondout Creek as technological advances shifted resource distribution towards quicker railroad lines. However, tourism flourished due to its picturesque landscapes of Catskill Mountains backdrop featuring falls such as Lower and Upper High Falls—both iconic landmarks for visitors today seeking natural beauty or outdoor activities like kayaking in summer months. In addition to offering recreational opportunities close by cities including New Paltz which became a hub fostering local food movements through accessibly farmland, Rondout Creek sustains native species habitats along with maintaining critical water quality due notably from upstream measures helping ensure clean drinkin

This enterprise provided the family with a comfortable living and enabled them to support their children’s education. Selma was educated at local schools in Indianapolis and later attended Butler University, which at that time offered a robust curriculum for women, including courses in literature, languages, science, and philosophy. Her education laid a foundation for her future roles as an educator and writer. After completing her studies, Selma began teaching in the Indianapolis Public Schools, where she was known for her dedication to her students and teaching methods. Her early life experiences, combined with her family's cultural background and stability, influenced her appreciation of literature, and nature—interests that would later converge beautifully at the House of the Singing Winds.

texts.full_text: sounds like you had a bit of an adventure with your Mac! Let's address your questions one by one. ### Swap Files Swap files on macOS are temporary files that the system uses to store data when it runs out of RAM. They are not user-generated files and are automatically managed by the operating system. When you delete them, macOS will recreate them as needed. So, no need to worry about them coming back; they will be recreated once your system needs them again. ### System Performance The slowdown and crashes you experienced could be due to several factors: 1. Memory Usage: Having large files open can indeed slow down a system, especially if it's low on RAM. 2. Background Processes: Some background processes might have been running, consuming resources. 3. Software Issues: Software bugs or glitches can also cause crashes. ### Time Machine Backup If you're concerned about data loss or want to restore your system to a previous state, using Time Machine is a good idea. However, in this case, since swap files are automatically managed by the system, restoring from Time Machine isn't necessary for them. ### Reboot vs. Restart Yes, "reboot" and "restart" mean the same thing. It's simply restarting your computer to clear out any temporary files and reset running processes. ### If You Need to Reboot If you're facing performance issues that persist, a reboot might help. However, if you have specific concerns about losing data or needing to reinstall software, here are some steps: 1. Check Running Processes: Use Activity Monitor (found in Applications > Utilities) to see what's using up resources. 2. Close Unnecessary Programs: Close any programs that aren't essential. 3. Update Software: Ensure your macOS and applications are up to date. 4. Disk Utility: Run Disk Utility (also in Applications > Utilities) to check for disk errors. If you're still facing issues after a reboot, it might be worth checking the Console app (found in Applications > Utilities) for any error messages that could give more insight into what's happening. texts.full_text: ortunity when former Vikings offensive coordinator and current Giants coach Pat Shurmur called for a handoff to running back Jon HilliHere is the completed text: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. — Dalvin Cook had his fourth 100-yard rushing game of the season on Sunday in the Vikings’ 28-10 victory over the Giants at MetLife Stadium, but that wasn’t the running back’s only involvement in the offensive mix. I think of Sherman Alexie's books or Dickens' and they could take no place else but where they're set. I'm so psyched to polish my historical and send it out. Probably next year. I had the chance to catch up with old friends and new. Here I am with Buffy Silverman who is taking a sabbatical with her husband in NC. My husband said that I've met my match. We're both smallish, NF writers. I've known Buffy online for quite some time, so it was great to meet her. Last year, I sat across from Ann Eisenstein at supper, aNd this year it was lovely to cement that friendship. Samantha Bell introduced me and I'm so thrilled she has a new picture book out. And last but not least, my lovely and trusted writing partner, Deana, who made sure we arrived at the conference and returned home in time for choir practice with plenty of time to spare. Solemn High Mass was heavenly. I managed to make all new mistakes, but the choirs of angels were there to help us. Now it's time to buckle down and prepare the Faure Requiem Mass. Your snippet presents an example where you insert new objects into two entities (`FailedBankInfo` & `FailedBatchedTransactionDetails`) within a managed object context, which later attempts to save these changes persistently. One key thing to note about CoreData isn't that it's intended for heavy data persistence as its name might suggest. It's actually more of a high-level abstraction layer over the SQLite (or other storage backends) providing Object-Relational Mapping (ORM), simplified database migrations, etc., making application code cleaner and developer productivity higher. Life and work Chute's first, and best known, novel, The Beans of Egypt, Maine, was published in 1985 and made into a 1994 film of the same name, directed by Jennifer Warren. Chute's next two books, Letourneau's Used Auto Parts (1988) and Merry Men (1994), are also set in the town of Egypt, Maine, as are the books in the "Heart's Content" series (The School on Heart's Content Road [2008], Treat Us Like Dogs and We Will Become Wolves [2014], and The Recipe for Revolution [2020]). Her 1999 novel Snow Man deals with the underground militia movement, something that Chute has devoted moreand more of her time and energy to. The School on Heart's Content Road, Treat Us Like Dogs and We Will Become Wolves, and The Recipe for Revolution, the first three books of the Heart's Content series, and the first three books of the Beans of Egypt, Maine series, The Beans of Egypt, Maine, Letourneau's Used Auto Parts, and Merry Men, and the nonfiction books The Political and Political and The Second Amendment, are available for purchase as e-books and as paperbacks on Amazon, and as e-books on Amazon, Kobo, and Nook. Carolyn Chute and her husband, the journalist and author Bill Chute, live on a farm in the woods in a house they built in the back woods in the small, western Maine town of Parsonsfield, Maine. Chute, a Second Amendment advocate, is the author of a 2010 nonfiction book, The Second Amendment, and is a regular contributor on the Second Amendment to the website of the Maine Gun Safety Coalition. Chute is the author of a 2010 nonfiction book, The Political, and Political, a book of advice to the populist political activist. texts.full_text: Class A California League. He entered military service in March 1942, at Fort McArthur in San Pedro, California, and having attended infantry school at Fort Benning, Georgia, he served with the Army Air Force at Barksdale Field, California, where he managed the baseball team. Mazzeo attained the rank of captain and was awarded the Silver Star for gallantry, while being wounded twice during the Italian campaign. Mazzeo was discharged from service in October 1945, and returned to umpiring in the Pacific Coast League in 1946, and remained until quitting to go back to his circulation department job at a Los Angeles newspaper in November 1947. However, he didn't sever all ties with baseball. In 1950, he had an uncredited role as an umpire in the 1950 William Bendix baseball movie, "Kill The Umpire." During the 1970s, while living in Burbank, California, he worked as a scout for the Kansas City Royals. Phil Mazzeo passed away on June 23, 1987, in Los Angeles, California. He was 77 years old and is buried at Forest Lawn Memorial Park in Los Angeles. To aid in objectively ascertaining the competency normally expected of attorneys practicing criminal law, we have suggested that courts should refer to precedent from state and federal courts, the American Bar Association Standards for Criminal Justice, and in some instances expert testimony about the conduct in issue. See Marzullo v. Maryland, 561 F.2d 540, 544 (4th Cir. 1977). 73 In Coles v. Peyton, 389 F.2d 224, 226 (4th Cir. 1968), speaking of appointed counsel, we said: 74 Counsel for an indigent defendant should be appointed promptly. Counsel should be afforded a reasonable opportunity to prepare to defend an accused. counsel must confer with his client without undue delay and as often as necessary, to advise him of his rights and to elicit matters of defense or to ascertain that potential defenses are unavailable. Counsel must conduct appropriate investigations, both factual and legal, to determine if matters of defense can be developed, and to allow himself enough time for reflection and preparation for trial. 75 Coles 's requirements are also applicable to retained counsel. See Cuyler v. Sullivan, 446 U.S. 335, 100 S.Ct. 1708, 1715-16, 64 L.Ed.2d 333 (1980); Stokes v. Peyton, 437 F.2d 131, 136-37 (4th Cir. 1970). In Marzullo, 561 F.2d at 544, we referred to the Coles standard as "an objective description of the competency normally demanded of counsel in certain aspects of their service." The ABA Code of Professional ResponsibiliTy, E.C. 2-30, cautions that a lawyer should not accept employment when he is unable to render competent service. Moreover, ABA Standards for Criminal Justice § 4-1.2(d) state that a lawyer "should not accept more employment than (he) can discharge within the spirit of the constitutional mandate for speedy trial and the limits of the lawyer's capacity for effective representation."
